title: Police: Mass. man allegedly stabbed friend during fight over woman at Miami Beach bachelor party
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Olajuwon Shawn Dickerson, 32, from Marston Mills, was arrested after allegedly stabbing his friend Brian Gordon in Miami Beach.
The alleged stabbing occurred at a bachelor party over a dispute involving women met at bars on Ocean Drive.
Dickerson was charged with aggravated battery and is held without bond; both men were treated for their injuries.

title: 4 takeaways from the Revolution’s 3-1 loss to St. Louis City SC
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: New England Revolution lost 3-1 to St. Louis City SC, marking their third defeat in four games.
St. Louis City SC took the lead with goals by Chris Durkin and Marcel Hartel after equalizing in the 29th minute.
Forward Leo Campana exited the game with a new injury, unrelated to his previous two-and-a-half-week absence.

title: Don’t be fooled by this fake court notice scam in Mass., NH, authorities warn
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Authorities in Massachusetts and New Hampshire warn about fake court notice scams using errors like 'State of Massachuseetts.'
Fraudulent notices direct recipients to pay fines via QR code, listing fake details like judges and municipal court contacts.
Residents are advised not to provide personal details, instead verify through official court contacts and delete suspicious messages.

title: The trial of Kelsey Fitzsimmons begins this week. Here’s what to know.
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Kelsey Fitzsimmons, an off-duty North Andover officer, was shot by a colleague during an armed confrontation at her home.
Fitzsimmons faces charges of assault with a dangerous weapon and has pleaded not guilty. Her trial starts March 23.
Conflicting accounts of the shooting and allegations of misconduct have complicated the legal proceedings.

title: She’s running Boston for patients ‘rebuilding their lives step by step’
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Lina Sinisterra Mazariegos, from Colombia, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon for Spaulding Rehabilitation Hospital.
Mazariegos's running journey is inspired by her personal growth and her podcast, sharing resilience stories.
Her marathon effort supports patients at Spaulding Rehabilitation in rebuilding their lives step by step.

title: Review & Setlist: ‘We are foreign imports, so we have paid tariffs.’
author: Marc Hirsh
bullets: The Hives performed at House of Blues with opening act The Chats on March 20, 2026.
Frontman Howlin’ Pelle Almqvist led the band with energy, engaging the audience with interactive moments.
The setlist included fan favorites and new songs, showcasing their consistency and audience enthusiasm.

title: Cancer couldn’t stop her. Now she’s running the Boston Marathon again.
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Stephanie McHugh, 42, from Braintree, is running her third Boston Marathon for the New England Patriots Foundation.
After a Stage 3 breast cancer diagnosis in 2023, McHugh resumed running with support from her mentor Susan Hurley.
McHugh previously ran for the Joe Andruzzi Foundation in honor of her uncle, a retired Fire Chief who died of cancer.

title: Markey presses Meta on facial recognition risks in smart glasses
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Sen. Ed Markey and others question Meta's facial recognition in smart glasses, citing privacy and protest implications.
Senators demand Meta clarify plans by April 6 on collecting and protecting biometric data from smart glasses.
Concerns arise ahead of mass protests as facial recognition tech may identify and track individuals at rallies.

title: Debate in Quincy over funding for new firefighting gear after current gear tests positive for PFAS
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Quincy's City Council delayed the vote on a $2.65 million proposal to replace PFAS-containing firefighter gear.
The new funding would provide two PFAS-free turnout gear sets per firefighter, after recent tests found harmful chemicals in current gear.
Councilors cited cost and durability concerns for PFAS-free gear, while fire officials stress the health risks of existing equipment.

title: The battle over the aging Attleboro Zoo is getting wild
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Mayor DeSimone proposed transforming Attleboro's Capron Park Zoo into a nature reserve to address high repair costs.
The zoo needs $57 million for improvements and costs $900,000 annually from the city's general fund.
The transition plan faces criticism, with debate ongoing about the zoo's future and potential closure.
